name: nan
mechanism: Proposed mechanism: DMT acts as a 5-HT2A agonist; psychoactive effects enhanced by MAOIs
contraindications: Avoid in individuals with psychiatric disorders
safetyNotes: Safety not well characterized in current repository sources; avoid use in pregnancy/lactation and in people with major medical or psychiatric conditions without clinical supervision.
description: Acacia confusa is a tree native to Southeast Asia used traditionally for spiritual and healing purposes. It contains psychoactive tryptamines like DMT.
dosage: Traditional root bark brew: 5–15g dried bark (not medically recommended)
preparation: Brewed as part of analog ayahuasca with MAOI-containing plants
summary: Acacia confusa entry normalized to baseline; key clinical and pharmacology fields are unresolved where repository evidence is limited.
